title: Portfolio item metadata and custom fields schema
topic: Portfolio item field catalogue, custom fields design, discussions system
summary: Field catalogue for all portfolio item metadata fields, gap analysis vs current schema, custom fields table design, and discussion system outline.
content:
Section A — Field Catalogue

Every field listed below is specified for the Vector portfolio item model. Rally equivalents are drawn from the Broadcom Rally portfolio item fields documentation. Types reflect the intended Postgres column or computed nature.

Field nameTypeDescriptionRally equivalentNotes
idUUID (PK)System-assigned unique identifier. Rendered as a human-readable tag at display time (e.g. FT-00000042) using key_num + the current tag from portfolio_item_types. The UUID never changes; the display tag may change if the type is renamed.ID (FormattedID)UUID is the stable identity. Display tag is computed, not stored on the row.
archivedboolean (derived from archived_at IS NOT NULL)Marks the portfolio item as archived. Archived items are excluded from live queries (WHERE archived_at IS NULL) but retained indefinitely for audit. Soft-archive only — no hard deletes.ArchivedStored as archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ (NULL = live). A boolean view column or computed field at read time. Consistent with the schema invariant in all business tables.
blockedbooleanIndicates the item is currently blocked. Auto-set true if any direct child item is marked blocked (rollup from execution layer). Can also be set manually by the owner or padmin.Blocked / StatusRally uses a separate Status field (Ready/Blocked) alongside the Blocked boolean. In Vector these collapse to a single blocked boolean. Rollup from children is computed, not stored — stored value reflects the item-level flag; a view or query aggregates child states.
blocked_reasonTEXT (nullable)Free-text explanation of why the item is blocked. Displayed alongside the blocked indicator. Required when blocked = true is set manually; optional on rollup-derived blocks.Blocked ReasonConditional display: only shown when blocked = true. Max recommended length 1 000 chars.
count_child_defectsINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total count of defect-type execution items directly parented to this portfolio item. Computed at reporting time or incrementally via trigger on child writes. Not stored as a durable column unless a materialised rollup strategy is chosen.Defect Count RollupRally stores this as a read-only rollup field. In Vector, prefer computing on read for correctness; materialise if query latency demands it.
count_child_user_storiesINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total count of user-story-type execution items parented (directly or via leaf resolution) to this portfolio item.Leaf Story CountRally distinguishes Leaf Story Count (direct) from rolled-up counts. Vector should clarify whether this is direct children only or full subtree. Recommend: direct only, with separate rollup queries for subtree.
count_dependantsINTEGER (computed)Count of other portfolio items or execution items that list this item as a dependency (i.e. this item blocks them). Computed from a dependency join table. Not a stored column.DependenciesRally shows a dependencies count and links to a detail view. In Vector the underlying table will be a portfolio_item_dependencies or item_dependencies join table (not yet built). This field is the count of rows where this item is the blocker.
count_rollup_defectINTEGER (computed / materialised)Accepted defect count rolled up from all descendant portfolio items and their execution children. Equivalent to Rally's Accepted Defect Count Rollup — counts defects in terminal/accepted state across the full subtree.Accepted Defect Count RollupRollup across hierarchy. Expensive to compute live at deep nesting; candidate for nightly materialisation or event-driven increment.
count_rollup_defectsINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total defect count (all states) rolled up from all descendant portfolio items. Distinct from count_rollup_defect (accepted only) — this covers all defects regardless of state.Defect Count Rollup (Total)Pair with count_rollup_defect (accepted) to derive a percent-done-by-defect metric.
count_rollup_estimationNUMERIC (computed / materialised)Total story-point estimate rolled up from all descendant execution items (all states). Used as the denominator in percent-done-by-estimate calculations.Total Estimate Rollup / Leaf Story Plan Estimate TotalRally stores as integer story points. Vector may use NUMERIC(10,2) to accommodate fractional estimates. Recomputed whenever a child estimate changes.
count_rollup_risksINTEGER (computed)Count of risk records linked to this portfolio item and its descendants. Computed from the risk join table. Surfaces portfolio-level risk exposure at a glance.No direct equivalent (Rally has Risk Score, not a risk count rollup)Vector-specific field. Distinct from risk_score (which is a weighted scoring field). This is a raw count of open risk records.
date_createdTIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()Timestamp at which the portfolio item row was inserted. System-generated; never editable.Creation DateMaps directly to the standard created_at column present on all Vector business tables.
date_work_acceptedTIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)The latest date on which any child execution item moved to an Accepted/terminal state, representing when all committed work on this item was accepted. NULL if no child items have reached that state yet.Actual End DateRally auto-calculates from the last child item reaching Accepted. In Vector: query MAX(transitioned_at) FROM item_state_history WHERE canonical_code = 'done' AND item_id IN (children). May be materialised for performance.
date_work_planned_finishDATE (nullable)User-specified target completion date. Not enforced by the system — it is a planning target, not a hard deadline. Used in roadmap views and for late-item highlighting.Planned End DateStored as DATE (not TIMESTAMPTZ) — plans are date-granular, not time-granular. Nullable: not all items have defined end dates.
date_work_planned_startDATE (nullable)User-specified target start date. Used in roadmap / Gantt views to position the item on a timeline.Planned Start DateDATE, nullable. Validation: if both are set, date_work_planned_start <= date_work_planned_finish.
date_work_startedTIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)The earliest date on which any child execution item moved to an In-Progress (active) state, representing when delivery work actually began on this item.Actual Start DateRally auto-calculates from the first child item reaching In Progress. In Vector: query MIN(transitioned_at) FROM item_state_history WHERE canonical_code = 'in_progress' AND item_id IN (children).
descriptionTEXT (nullable)Rich-text description of the portfolio item — its purpose, scope, and context. Supports markdown or an agreed rich-text format. Not to be confused with notes or comments.DescriptionRally supports embedded images, tables, and rich formatting up to the field limit. In Vector, store as text (markdown or HTML) and render on the client. Max recommended: 64 KB.
acceptance_criteriaTEXT (nullable)Rich-text acceptance criteria for the portfolio item — the conditions that must be met for the item to be considered done. Shared field across portfolio items, user stories, and defects.Acceptance CriteriaStore as markdown or HTML. Rendered on the client. Applies to all strategic item types where a team must verify a done state.
discussions— (separate system)Portfolio items participate in a threaded discussion system. This is not a count column — it is a relationship to a separate discussion_posts table set. See Section D for the full schema outline.DiscussionsRally's Discussions field is a collaboration widget showing time-ordered flat comments (not threaded). Vector will exceed this with threading, reactions, and mentions. The field name on the item record is simply a pointer / presence indicator; all data lives in the discussion tables.
estimate_initialTEXT (nullable)Initial T-shirt size estimate set during planning: one of XS, S, M, L, XL (or a configurable vocabulary). Represents the rough order-of-magnitude sizing at intake before detailed breakdown.Preliminary EstimateRally uses S/M/L/XL with admin-defined point equivalents. In Vector, store as TEXT with a CHECK constraint against the configured vocabulary. Consider a separate estimate_size_options catalogue if vocabulary is per-subscription.
estimate_updatedNUMERIC(10,2) (nullable)Refined story-point estimate set after breakdown. Replaces estimate_initial as the delivery-planning figure once the team has enough detail. Used in rollup calculations.Refined EstimateRally also tracks Refined Work Item Count Estimate (count-based, non-point). Vector may add a count-based companion field (estimate_updated_count) in future if needed.
done_by_story_countNUMERIC(5,2) (computed)Percentage of child user stories in Accepted/terminal state vs. total child user stories. Computed at reporting time as (accepted_story_count / total_story_count) * 100. NULL if no child stories exist.Percent Done by Story Count / Accepted Leaf Story CountThis is the "done by story count" percentage, not a raw count. Computed, not stored. The field name in the spec uses "count" to mean the story-count-based percentage metric.
flow_stateUUID FK -> item_type_states.id (nullable)The portfolio item's current position in the flow state board. References a row in item_type_states which is scoped per (subscription, item type). This is the Kanban column position — not a fixed enum, but a pointer to the subscription's configured workflow states.State (Portfolio Kanban column)Vector uses the existing item_type_states / canonical_states / item_type_transition_edges system. Transitions are validated against item_type_transition_edges before committing. History is recorded in item_state_history.
flow_state_change_update_dateTIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)Timestamp of the most recent flow state transition on this item. Maintained by the state-change handler (not a DB trigger). Enables "time in current state" calculations: NOW() - flow_state_change_update_date.State Changed DateEquivalent to Rally's read-only "State Changed Date". Updated atomically with flow_state in the same transaction. The full transition history is in item_state_history; this column is the cached "last changed at" for fast queries.
flow_state_change_ownerUUID FK -> users.id (nullable)The user who last changed the flow state of this item. Cached alongside flow_state_change_update_date for display without joining item_state_history.No direct Rally equivalent (implicit via Revision History)Vector-specific cached field. The authoritative record of who changed state and when is in item_state_history.transitioned_by + transitioned_at; this is a convenience denormalization for the item detail view.
hierarchy_child— (relationship)Collection of child portfolio items directly nested under this item. Not a stored column — derived from the parent_id FK on lower-level items pointing up to this one.ChildrenRendered in the UI as a list of linked child items. Query: SELECT * FROM portfolio_items WHERE parent_id = $this_id AND archived_at IS NULL.
hierarchy_parentUUID FK -> parent item (nullable)Reference to the parent portfolio item in the hierarchy. NULL means the item is at the top of its hierarchy level. ON DELETE RESTRICT — parent must be archived before child.Parent and HierarchyCurrently modelled as portfolio.workspace_id and product.parent_portfolio_id in the existing schema. When a unified portfolio_items table is introduced, this becomes a self-referencing parent_id UUID REFERENCES portfolio_items(id).
lidentifier_colourTEXT (nullable)Hex colour code (e.g. #3B82F6) for the visual label badge displayed on this item in board and list views. Part of the lidentifier (visual display identifier) system — a colour-coded badge that makes item types instantly recognisable at a glance.No direct Rally equivalent (Rally uses Portfolio Item Type colour coding)Vector-specific visual system. Colour is configurable per item type at the subscription level, or overridden per item. Store as TEXT with a CHECK constraint: colour ~* '^#[0-9a-f]{6}$'.
lidentifier_labelsTEXT[] (nullable)Array of short display labels attached to the item badge (e.g. ["Strategic", "Q3"]). Part of the lidentifier system. Labels are free-text tags applied to the visual identifier for filtering and grouping in board views.Tags (partial)Distinct from lidentifier_tags — labels are displayed on the badge itself (visible on the card); tags are metadata for filtering that may not be visible on the card face.
lidentifier_tagsTEXT[] (nullable)Array of metadata tags used for filtering, search, and reporting. Not necessarily displayed on the card face. Part of the lidentifier system's broader metadata layer.TagsRally's Tags field is a multi-select enabling filtering and reporting. In Vector, tags are stored as a Postgres array for simplicity (avoids a join table for MVP). Consider a normalised item_tags join table if tag vocabularies need to be subscription-managed.
lidentifier_typeUUID FK -> portfolio_item_types.id (NOT NULL)The portfolio item type classification — e.g. Feature, Initiative, Theme. References the subscription's portfolio_item_types catalogue. Determines the tag prefix used in the human-readable ID, the flow state workflow applied, and which custom fields are available.Portfolio Item TypeCurrently the type_id column on portfolio and product tables. The lidentifier type is the source of the colour, badge shape, and tag in the visual identifier system.
nameTEXT NOT NULLThe human-readable name of the portfolio item. Required. Must be meaningful and unique within its parent scope (uniqueness enforced at application layer or via partial unique index).NameCurrently present as name TEXT NOT NULL on portfolio and product tables.
name_authorUUID FK -> users.id (NOT NULL)The user who created the portfolio item. System-assigned at creation time; never editable. Equivalent to "Created By" in Rally.Created ByRally's Created By is read-only and system-assigned. In Vector: populated from session user on INSERT, never updated. Currently approximated by owner_user_id on portfolio/product which conflates creator and current owner.
name_ownerUUID FK -> users.id (NOT NULL)The user currently responsible for the portfolio item. Defaults to the creator at creation time; may be changed by padmin or the current owner. Drives assignment notifications and ownership views.OwnerCurrently owner_user_id on portfolio and product. ON DELETE RESTRICT — owner must be reassigned before the user can be deleted. Separate from name_author (creator) once that field is introduced.
risk_impactTEXT or INTEGER (nullable)The assessed impact level of the primary risk on this item if it materialises. Typically a categorical scale: Low / Medium / High / Critical, or a 1–5 integer. Used with risk_probability to compute risk_score.No direct Rally equivalent (Rally has Risk Score as a single field)Vector splits risk into impact x probability to allow a calculated score. Impact stored as TEXT with CHECK in ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical') or as INTEGER 1–5.
risk_probabilityTEXT or INTEGER (nullable)The assessed probability that the primary risk will occur: Low / Medium / High, or a percentage. Combined with risk_impact to produce risk_score.No direct Rally equivalentSame storage recommendation as risk_impact. A simple 3x3 or 5x5 risk matrix maps (impact, probability) to score.
risk_scoreNUMERIC(5,2) (nullable, computed or manually set)Overall delivery risk assessment score for this portfolio item. May be computed from risk_impact x risk_probability or set manually by the padmin. Higher scores = higher risk. Used for risk-sorted portfolio views.Risk ScoreRally's Risk Score is a single numeric field set by the organisation. Vector extends this with the impact/probability decomposition above. Note: field was listed as "rick_score" in the spec — confirmed typo; the correct name is risk_score.
strategic_investment_groupTEXT (nullable)The investment area or strategic bucket this item belongs to — e.g. "Growth", "User Reach", "Efficiency", "Compliance". Used to group items by investment theme in portfolio views and reporting. Controlled vocabulary per subscription.Investment CategoryRally's Investment Category is a dropdown for budget allocation alignment. Vector's strategic_investment_group serves the same purpose. Store as TEXT with an optional FK to a strategic_groups catalogue table (not yet built); for MVP, store free-text with a CHECK against a configurable list.
strategic_investment_weightTEXT (nullable)The relative weight or priority of this item within its strategic_investment_group: Low / Medium / High. Used to scale items within a group for prioritisation and resource allocation views without overriding the group assignment.No direct Rally equivalent (Value Score is the closest)Vector-specific field. Stored as TEXT CHECK in ('low', 'medium', 'high'). Could be extended to a numeric weight (0.0–1.0) in a future iteration.
strategic_item_typeTEXT (nullable)Classification of the nature of the investment or work this item represents — e.g. "New Capability", "Technical Debt", "Compliance", "Maintenance". Complements the strategic investment group with a type-of-work dimension.No direct Rally equivalentVector-specific. Enables filtering and reporting by type of strategic work. Stored as TEXT; controlled vocabulary per subscription or globally seeded. Distinct from lidentifier_type (which is the hierarchy level type, e.g. Feature vs Initiative).
value_stream_identifierTEXT or UUID FK (nullable)Associates the portfolio item with a value stream — the end-to-end flow of value delivery from concept to customer. Used for value stream mapping and SAFe-aligned views. May reference a future value_streams catalogue table.No direct Rally equivalent (Rally uses Project/Team associations)Vector-specific field targeting organisations using value stream management. For MVP, store as TEXT (free-form value stream name). Migrate to a UUID FK when a value_streams table is built.

Section B — Current Vector Schema

What currently exists

The current mmff_vector schema (migrations 001–041 applied as of 2026-04-29) has the following tables that form the portfolio item foundation:

  • portfolio — represents a portfolio grouping within a workspace. Columns: id, subscription_id, workspace_id, type_id (nullable FK to portfolio_item_types), key_num, name, owner_user_id, archived_at, created_at, updated_at.
  • product — the smallest portfolio-stack node; child of a portfolio or direct child of a workspace. Same shape as portfolio plus parent_portfolio_id.
  • portfolio_item_types — per-subscription catalogue of portfolio item type names and tags (e.g. Feature, Initiative). Columns: id, subscription_id, name, tag, sort_order, archived_at, created_at, updated_at.
  • item_type_states — per-(subscription, item-type) workflow states. Supports the flow state system.
  • item_type_transition_edges — allowed state transitions per item type. Used to validate flow state moves.
  • item_state_history — append-only state-change journal. Awaiting work-item tables to become active.
  • entity_stakeholders — polymorphic stakeholder assignments on portfolio entities.
  • Adoption mirror tables — subscription_layers, subscription_workflows, etc. These mirror the mmff_library portfolio model bundle but are not directly the item-metadata tables.

Gap analysis vs. the field catalogue

The following fields from Section A have no current column or table in the schema:

Field groupFields absentImpact
Blockingblocked, blocked_reasonNo blocker model at all on portfolio items
Rollup countscount_child_defects, count_child_user_stories, count_dependants, count_rollup_defect, count_rollup_defects, count_rollup_estimation, count_rollup_risksNo work-item tables yet; rollups cannot be computed
Datesdate_work_accepted, date_work_planned_finish, date_work_planned_start, date_work_startedOnly created_at/updated_at exist; no planning dates
Contentdescription, estimate_initial, estimate_updatedNo description or estimation fields on current tables
Flow state metadataflow_state, flow_state_change_update_date, flow_state_change_ownerState system tables exist but no FK from portfolio/product to current state
Author vs. owner splitname_authorCurrent owner_user_id conflates creator and owner
Riskrisk_impact, risk_probability, risk_scoreNo risk fields
Strategicstrategic_investment_group, strategic_investment_weight, strategic_item_type, value_stream_identifierNo strategic classification fields
Visual identifierlidentifier_colour, lidentifier_labels, lidentifier_tagsNo colour/label/tag system on items
DiscussionsEntire discussion systemNo discussion tables of any kind
Custom fieldsEntire custom field systemNo custom field tables of any kind
Progress / done metricsdone_by_story_countComputable once work-item tables exist; no storage needed

Pre-requisite: a unified portfolio_items table

The current two-table split (portfolio and product) reflects hierarchy levels, not item types. When item-level metadata is added, the recommended path is to introduce a unified portfolio_items table with a self-referencing parent_id, and migrate portfolio and product to rows in that table. This aligns with the schema's existing portfolio_item_types catalogue and the adoption mirror pattern.

Section C — Custom Fields Design

Scope model

Custom fields are scoped to the intersection of: workspace x portfolio item type x hierarchy level. For example, "all Features in Workspace 1" can have a different custom schema from "all Features in Workspace 2", or from "all Initiatives in Workspace 1". This means the field definition is keyed on (workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id) — hierarchy level is implicit in the item type.

padmin controls field definitions (create, edit, delete, reorder). Users fill field values. gadmin has no special role in the custom field schema beyond their normal system access.

Supported field types

The custom_field_type column should support the following vocabulary:

  • text — single-line free text (max 500 chars)
  • textarea — multi-line free text (max 4 000 chars)
  • number — integer or decimal numeric value
  • boolean — true/false checkbox
  • date — calendar date (no time component)
  • select — single selection from a defined option list
  • multiselect — multiple selections from a defined option list
  • user — reference to a users.id within the subscription
  • url — validated URL string

Value storage trade-offs

StrategyProsCons
EAV (one row per field value, typed columns)Type-safe queries; easy to index individual field values; clean FK to field definitionWide result sets require pivoting; complex queries; joins multiply with field count
JSONB blob on item rowSimple; no joins; easy to add fields without migrationNo type enforcement at DB level; hard to index individual fields; schema hidden inside JSON
Single values table with a value_jsonb columnOne row per (item, field definition); flexible type; GIN indexable; easy to query a specific item's full custom data in one row per fieldStill needs type coercion at application layer; value cannot be DB-typed

Recommended: hybrid EAV with typed value columns. Store one row per (item, field definition) in portfolio_item_field_values, with separate nullable typed columns (value_text, value_number, value_boolean, value_date, value_jsonb). The application writes to the correct typed column based on custom_field_type; the DB schema enforces no cross-column constraint (application layer enforces single-column population). This gives indexability per type while avoiding the full EAV pivot complexity.

SQL CREATE TABLE statements

-- Table 1: Custom field definitions (schema layer)
-- One row per defined field, scoped to (workspace, portfolio item type).
-- padmin creates and manages these; users cannot modify definitions.

CREATE TABLE portfolio_item_field_definitions (
    id                      UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id         UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    workspace_id            UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES workspace(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    portfolio_item_type_id  UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES portfolio_item_types(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    custom_field_label      TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (length(trim(custom_field_label)) > 0),
    custom_field_description TEXT,
    custom_field_type       TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (custom_field_type IN (
                                'text', 'textarea', 'number', 'boolean',
                                'date', 'select', 'multiselect', 'user', 'url'
                            )),
    custom_field_required   BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    custom_field_position   INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    custom_field_creator    UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    archived_at             TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT pifd_label_unique UNIQUE (workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id, custom_field_label)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pifd_subscription   ON portfolio_item_field_definitions(subscription_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_pifd_workspace_type ON portfolio_item_field_definitions(workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id)
    WHERE archived_at IS NULL;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_pifd_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON portfolio_item_field_definitions
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();


-- Table 2: Custom field values (data layer)
-- One row per (portfolio item, field definition).
-- Typed nullable columns; only the column matching the definition type is populated.

CREATE TABLE portfolio_item_field_values (
    id                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    portfolio_item_id   UUID        NOT NULL,
    field_definition_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES portfolio_item_field_definitions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    value_text          TEXT,
    value_number        NUMERIC(18, 4),
    value_boolean       BOOLEAN,
    value_date          DATE,
    value_jsonb         JSONB,

    set_by              UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT pifv_item_field_unique UNIQUE (portfolio_item_id, field_definition_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pifv_subscription ON portfolio_item_field_values(subscription_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_pifv_item         ON portfolio_item_field_values(portfolio_item_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_pifv_definition   ON portfolio_item_field_values(field_definition_id);

CREATE TRIGGER trg_pifv_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON portfolio_item_field_values
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();


-- Table 3: Options for select/multiselect types.

CREATE TABLE portfolio_item_field_options (
    id            UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    definition_id UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES portfolio_item_field_definitions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label         TEXT    NOT NULL CHECK (length(trim(label)) > 0),
    colour        TEXT,
    sort_order    INT     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    archived_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT pifo_label_unique UNIQUE (definition_id, label)
);

Key design decisions

  • Scope via (workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id) — not (subscription_id, type_id) — because padmin may want different schemas per workspace even for the same item type.
  • Soft-archive definitions — never hard-delete a field definition; archive it. Existing values are retained for history; the field simply stops appearing in the form.
  • ON DELETE RESTRICT on definition FK in values table — prevents orphaned values when a definition is accidentally deleted; padmin must archive the definition instead.
  • Position (sort order) is per-scope — the custom_field_position column controls display order within the form for a given (workspace, item type).
  • Value uniqueness — UNIQUE (portfolio_item_id, field_definition_id) enforces one value row per field per item. UPSERT semantics at write time (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE).
  • No null value rows — if a field has no value for an item, no row is inserted. The read path LEFT JOINs and treats missing rows as null values.

Section D — Discussions System

Overview

The discussions system attaches a feature-rich threaded collaboration space to any portfolio item (and, by extension, to execution items). It goes beyond Rally's flat comment list by adding: threading (replies), reactions, @mentions, attachment pointers, and read receipts.

Threading model

Flat-with-replies: top-level posts have parent_post_id = NULL; replies reference a top-level post via parent_post_id. Maximum one level of nesting (reply to a post, but not a reply to a reply). This is the Discord/Slack thread model — avoids the complexity of infinite recursion while providing richer collaboration than Rally's fully flat list.

Schema outline

-- discussion_threads: one thread per portfolio item (or execution item)
CREATE TABLE discussion_threads (
    id              UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    entity_kind     TEXT    NOT NULL CHECK (entity_kind IN ('portfolio_item', 'execution_item')),
    entity_id       UUID    NOT NULL,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (entity_kind, entity_id)
);

-- discussion_posts: individual posts and replies within a thread
CREATE TABLE discussion_posts (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    thread_id       UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES discussion_threads(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    parent_post_id  UUID        REFERENCES discussion_posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    author_id       UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    body            TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (length(trim(body)) > 0),
    edited_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
    deleted_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- discussion_reactions: emoji reactions on posts
CREATE TABLE discussion_reactions (
    id          UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id     UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES discussion_posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id     UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    emoji       TEXT    NOT NULL CHECK (length(emoji) > 0),
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id, emoji)
);

-- discussion_mentions: @mention index for notification dispatch
CREATE TABLE discussion_mentions (
    id                UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id           UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES discussion_posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    mentioned_user_id UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    notified_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (post_id, mentioned_user_id)
);

-- discussion_attachments: file metadata pointers (files stored in blob store)
CREATE TABLE discussion_attachments (
    id              UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id         UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES discussion_posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    file_name       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    file_size_bytes BIGINT,
    mime_type       TEXT,
    storage_key     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    uploaded_by     UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- discussion_read_receipts: per-user last-read position in a thread
CREATE TABLE discussion_read_receipts (
    thread_id         UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES discussion_threads(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id           UUID    NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    last_read_post_id UUID    REFERENCES discussion_posts(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    last_read_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    PRIMARY KEY (thread_id, user_id)
);

Key design decisions

  • One thread per item — simplicity; all collaboration on an item lives in one place. If multi-topic threads are needed later, introduce a topic column on discussion_threads.
  • Flat-with-one-level-of-replies — replies to replies are not permitted. Enforce at application layer: if parent_post_id is set, validate that the referenced post itself has parent_post_id = NULL.
  • Soft-delete posts — deleted_at is set; body is replaced with [deleted] at read time. Reactions and replies to deleted posts remain visible. Never hard-delete discussion content.
  • Attachments as storage pointers only — the DB row stores the storage_key (e.g. an S3 key) and metadata. Signed URL generation happens at read time in the API layer. This keeps the DB lean and avoids storing binary data.
  • Read receipts as UPSERT — the discussion_read_receipts table is updated on each view: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET last_read_post_id, last_read_at = now(). Drives unread count badges on board cards.
  • Mentions index for notifications — discussion_mentions is a parsed index written at post-creation time by the application (parse @username from body, resolve to user_id, insert). A background worker reads WHERE notified_at IS NULL and dispatches notifications, then sets notified_at.
  • No full-text search index included here — GIN index on discussion_posts.body using to_tsvector is recommended when search is required, but omitted from this outline as a future concern.
  • subscription_id on posts only — child tables (reactions, mentions, attachments, read receipts) cascade from posts; subscription isolation is enforced at the posts level via joins. Add subscription_id to child tables only if direct queries without a thread/post join are required.
